  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c1", "name": "AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF)", "aliases": ["AI RMF", "AI RMF Core"], "description": "The NIST framework whose Core outcomes (Tables 1-4 in AI RMF 1.0) the Playbook provides suggested actions for achieving.", "grounding_span": "the AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF) Core", "location": "About the Playbook"},
    {"id": "c2", "name": "Govern function", "aliases": ["Govern"], "description": "AI RMF function ensuring policies, processes, procedures and practices for mapping, measuring and managing AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.", "grounding_span": "the mapping, measuring and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented", "location": "GOVERN"},
    {"id": "c3", "name": "Map function", "aliases": ["Map"], "description": "AI RMF function in which context is established and understood.", "grounding_span": "Context is established and understood.", "location": "MAP"},
    {"id": "c4", "name": "Measure function", "aliases": ["Measure"], "description": "AI RMF function in which appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.", "grounding_span": "Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.", "location": "MEASURE"},
    {"id": "c5", "name": "Manage function", "aliases": ["Manage"], "description": "AI RMF function in which AI risks based on analytical output from Map and Measure are prioritized, responded to, and managed.", "grounding_span": "are prioritized, responded to, and managed.", "location": "MANAGE"},
    {"id": "c6", "name": "AI risk", "aliases": ["AI risks"], "description": "Risks associated with the design, development, deployment, and use of AI systems that the framework seeks to map, measure, and manage.", "grounding_span": "managing of AI risks", "location": "GOVERN"},
    {"id": "c7", "name": "Legal and regulatory requirements", "aliases": [], "description": "Applicable legal and regulatory requirements involving AI that are to be understood, managed, and documented.", "grounding_span": "Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.", "location": "GOVERN 1.1"},
    {"id": "c8", "name": "Disparate impact", "aliases": [], "description": "A facially neutral policy or practice that disproportionately harms a group based on a protected trait.", "grounding_span": "such as disparate impact", "location": "GOVERN 1.1"},
    {"id": "c9", "name": "Disparate treatment", "aliases": ["intentional discrimination"], "description": "Legally prohibited intentional discrimination, in tension with some demographic-information-reliant debiasing techniques.", "grounding_span": "legal prohibitions on disparate", "location": "GOVERN 1.1"},
    {"id": "c10", "name": "Digital divide", "aliases": [], "description": "The phenomenon whereby some users lack consistent or reliable access to fundamental internet technologies.", "grounding_span": "a phenomenon widely described as the", "location": "GOVERN 1.1"},
    {"id": "c11", "name": "Trustworthy AI characteristics", "aliases": ["characteristics of trustworthy AI", "trustworthiness characteristics"], "description": "Characteristics of trustworthy AI to be integrated into organizational policies, processes, and procedures.", "grounding_span": "The characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into organizational policies, processes,", "location": "GOVERN 1.2"},
    {"id": "c12", "name": "Risk tolerance", "aliases": [], "description": "The level and type of risk an organization is willing to accept while conducting its mission and carrying out its strategy.", "grounding_span": "based on the organization's risk tolerance.", "location": "GOVERN 1.3"},
    {"id": "c13", "name": "Risk scale", "aliases": ["red-amber-green", "RAG"], "description": "A standardized scale (qualitative such as RAG, or via simulations/econometric approaches) onto which a risk score is placed.", "grounding_span": "This score is then placed on a risk scale.", "location": "GOVERN 1.3"},
    {"id": "c14", "name": "Impact assessment", "aliases": [], "description": "A common tool for understanding the severity of mapped risks and framing risks of a given algorithm or deployment.", "grounding_span": "Impact assessments are a common tool for understanding the severity of mapped risks.", "location": "GOVERN 1.3"},
    {"id": "c15", "name": "Continuous monitoring", "aliases": ["ongoing monitoring"], "description": "A risk management process for tracking unexpected issues and performance changes across the AI system lifecycle.", "grounding_span": "Continuous monitoring is a risk management process for tracking unexpected", "location": "GOVERN 1.5"},
    {"id": "c16", "name": "Incident response", "aliases": [], "description": "Commonly used IT-management process enabling real-time flagging of potential incidents and human adjudication.", "grounding_span": "Establishing and maintaining incident response plans", "location": "GOVERN 1.5"},
    {"id": "c17", "name": "Appeal and override", "aliases": [], "description": "Process enabling human adjudication of AI system outcomes.", "grounding_span": "\u201cappeal and override\u201d are commonly used processes", "location": "GOVERN 1.5"},
    {"id": "c18", "name": "AI system inventory", "aliases": ["model inventory"], "description": "An organized database of artifacts relating to an AI system or model, enabling a holistic view of organizational AI assets.", "grounding_span": "An AI system inventory is an organized database of artifacts relating to an AI system or", "location": "GOVERN 1.6"},
    {"id": "c19", "name": "Decommissioning", "aliases": ["phasing out"], "description": "Systematic and deliberate termination of AI systems in a manner that does not increase risks or decrease trustworthiness.", "grounding_span": "Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning and phasing out of AI systems", "location": "GOVERN 1.7"},
    {"id": "c20", "name": "Model risk management", "aliases": [], "description": "Traditional risk management approach for models from which AI risk management practices borrow (e.g., inventories, effective challenge).", "grounding_span": "traditional model risk management", "location": "GOVERN 1.6"},
    {"id": "c21", "name": "Three lines of defense", "aliases": [], "description": "Structure in which separate teams are accountable for different aspects of the system lifecycle such as development, risk management, and auditing.", "grounding_span": "implement three (or more) \u201clines of", "location": "GOVERN 4.1"},
    {"id": "c22", "name": "Effective challenge", "aliases": [], "description": "A culture-based practice encouraging critical thinking and questioning of important design and implementation decisions by empowered experts.", "grounding_span": "\u201ceffective challenge,\u201d is a", "location": "GOVERN 4.1"},
    {"id": "c23", "name": "Red-teaming", "aliases": ["red teaming"], "description": "Adversarial testing of AI systems under stress conditions to seek out failure modes or vulnerabilities, conducted by independent experts.", "grounding_span": "Red-teaming is another risk measurement and management approach.", "location": "GOVERN 4.1"},
    {"id": "c24", "name": "Whistleblower protections", "aliases": ["whistleblower policies"], "description": "Protections for insiders who report perceived serious problems with AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Establish whistleblower protections for insiders", "location": "GOVERN 4.1"},
    {"id": "c25", "name": "Diverse team", "aliases": ["team diversity"], "description": "A team with diversity of demographics, disciplines, experience, expertise, and backgrounds informing AI risk decisions.", "grounding_span": "is informed by a diverse team", "location": "GOVERN 3.1"},
    {"id": "c26", "name": "Human-AI configuration", "aliases": ["human-AI configurations"], "description": "Configurations where AI systems are explicitly designated and treated as team members in primarily human teams.", "grounding_span": "human-AI configurations (configurations", "location": "GOVERN 3.2"},
    {"id": "c27", "name": "Human oversight", "aliases": [], "description": "Defined, assessed, and documented processes for human oversight of AI systems in accordance with Govern policies.", "grounding_span": "Processes for human oversight are defined, assessed, and documented", "location": "MAP 3.5"},
    {"id": "c28", "name": "Human-AI teaming", "aliases": [], "description": "Configuration of humans and automation working together, a known source of difficulty in managing AI risks.", "grounding_span": "human-AI teaming", "location": "GOVERN 3.2"},
    {"id": "c29", "name": "Third-party risk", "aliases": ["third-party AI systems"], "description": "Risks associated with third-party entities, data, software, or hardware integrated into AI products or services.", "grounding_span": "AI risks associated with third-party", "location": "GOVERN 6.1"},
    {"id": "c30", "name": "Intellectual property infringement", "aliases": [], "description": "Risk of infringing a third party's intellectual property or other rights when using third-party components.", "grounding_span": "including risks of infringement of a third party\u2019s intellectual property or other", "location": "GOVERN 6.1"},
    {"id": "c31", "name": "Participatory stakeholder engagement", "aliases": [], "description": "A qualitative activity helping AI actors decide whether to pursue a project or how to design with impact in mind.", "grounding_span": "Participatory stakeholder engagement is one type of qualitative activity", "location": "GOVERN 5.1"},
    {"id": "c32", "name": "Participation washing", "aliases": [], "description": "The risk that consultative engagement becomes a perfunctory exercise lacking genuine influence.", "grounding_span": "\u201cparticipation washing.\u201d", "location": "GOVERN 5.1"},
    {"id": "c33", "name": "Residual risk", "aliases": ["negative residual risks"], "description": "The sum of all unmitigated risks, which may be accepted or transferred and affect downstream AI actors.", "grounding_span": "Negative residual risks (defined as the sum of all unmitigated risks)", "location": "MANAGE 1.4"},
    {"id": "c34", "name": "Risk (composite measure)", "aliases": [], "description": "The composite measure of an event's probability of occurring and the magnitude of the consequences of the corresponding events.", "grounding_span": "Risk refers to the composite measure of an event\u2019s probability of occurring and the", "location": "MANAGE 1.2"},
    {"id": "c35", "name": "Risk response options", "aliases": [], "description": "Options for responding to risk: mitigating, transferring, avoiding, or accepting.", "grounding_span": "Risk response options can include mitigating, transferring, avoiding, or accepting.", "location": "MANAGE 1.3"},
    {"id": "c36", "name": "Drift", "aliases": ["model drift", "data drift"], "description": "Evolution/shift of system performance and trustworthiness over time after deployment that can degrade an AI system's value.", "grounding_span": "This phenomenon, generally known as drift, can degrade", "location": "MANAGE 2.2"},
    {"id": "c37", "name": "Concept drift", "aliases": [], "description": "A failure mode difficult to identify using current standard AI testing processes.", "grounding_span": "Issues such as concept drift", "location": "GOVERN 4.3"},
    {"id": "c38", "name": "TEVV", "aliases": ["test, evaluation, validation and verification"], "description": "Test, evaluation, validation and verification protocols and metrics applied across the AI lifecycle.", "grounding_span": "test, evaluation, validation and verification (TEVV)", "location": "MANAGE 2.2"},
    {"id": "c39", "name": "Transfer learning", "aliases": [], "description": "Adapting an existing pre-trained model for use in a different but related application.", "grounding_span": "A common approach in AI development is transfer learning", "location": "MANAGE 3.2"},
    {"id": "c40", "name": "Pre-trained models", "aliases": [], "description": "Models trained on large datasets by third parties and adapted for development, whose use can make negative outcomes hard to anticipate.", "grounding_span": "Pre-trained models which are used for development are monitored", "location": "MANAGE 3.2"},
    {"id": "c41", "name": "Foundation models", "aliases": [], "description": "Third-party material required for system implementation and maintenance that must be inventoried.", "grounding_span": "foundation models,", "location": "MAP 4.1"},
    {"id": "c42", "name": "Context mapping", "aliases": [], "description": "The first step in delineating bounds of acceptable deployment, including examination of intended purpose, deployment setting, and expectations.", "grounding_span": "Context mapping is the first step in this", "location": "MAP 1.1"},
    {"id": "c43", "name": "Non-AI alternatives", "aliases": ["non-technology alternatives"], "description": "Semi-autonomous or mostly-manual methods considered as alternatives to AI to manage potential negative impacts.", "grounding_span": "consideration of non-AI or  non-technology", "location": "MAP 1.1"},
    {"id": "c44", "name": "Go/no-go decision", "aliases": [], "description": "A decision about whether to continue with development or deployment, informed by risk tolerances.", "grounding_span": "the clarity of \u201cgo/no-", "location": "MAP 1.3"},
    {"id": "c45", "name": "Socio-technical system", "aliases": ["socio-technical"], "description": "AI systems whose risks emerge from the interplay of technical decisions with use, operators, and social context.", "grounding_span": "Socio-technical AI risks emerge from the interplay", "location": "MAP 1.4"},
    {"id": "c46", "name": "Construct validity", "aliases": [], "description": "Demonstration that an indicator is measuring the concept it claims to measure.", "grounding_span": "also known as construct validity", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "c47", "name": "Internal validity", "aliases": [], "description": "Validity in which the relationship being tested is not influenced by other factors or variables.", "grounding_span": "internal validity (relationship being tested is not influenced by other factors or", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "c48", "name": "External validity", "aliases": [], "description": "Validity in which results are generalizable beyond the training condition.", "grounding_span": "external validity (results are generalizable beyond the training condition)", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "c49", "name": "Validity", "aliases": ["valid and reliable"], "description": "Property of an AI system being demonstrated to be valid and reliable, including construct, internal, and external forms.", "grounding_span": "The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be valid and reliable.", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "c50", "name": "Proxy", "aliases": ["proxy targets"], "description": "Indicators constructed to operationalize phenomena not directly observable or measurable, introducing validation risks.", "grounding_span": "the use of proxies or other indicators", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "c51", "name": "Off-label use", "aliases": [], "description": "Uses of AI systems for purposes outside intended scope, especially in high-risk settings.", "grounding_span": "for \u201coff-label\u201d purposes", "location": "MAP 1.5"},
    {"id": "c52", "name": "Human subjects protection", "aliases": [], "description": "Legally and disciplinarily required protection of human subjects involved in AI evaluations, including informed consent.", "grounding_span": "including human subject protection", "location": "MEASURE 2.2"},
    {"id": "c53", "name": "Disaggregated evaluation", "aliases": [], "description": "Evaluation methods broken out by race, age, gender, ethnicity, ability, region, etc., to improve real-world performance.", "grounding_span": "Utilize disaggregated evaluation methods", "location": "MEASURE 2.2"},
    {"id": "c54", "name": "Safety", "aliases": [], "description": "Property of an AI system being demonstrated to be safe and able to fail safely, particularly beyond its knowledge limits.", "grounding_span": "AI system is evaluated regularly for safety risks", "location": "MEASURE 2.6"},
    {"id": "c55", "name": "Chaos engineering", "aliases": [], "description": "Testing approach that exercises systems in extreme conditions to gauge unexpected responses.", "grounding_span": "Apply chaos engineering approaches to test systems in extreme conditions and", "location": "MEASURE 2.6"},
    {"id": "c56", "name": "Security", "aliases": [], "description": "Maintenance of confidentiality, integrity, and availability through protection mechanisms that prevent unauthorized access and use.", "grounding_span": "AI system security and resilience", "location": "MEASURE 2.7"},
    {"id": "c57", "name": "Resilience", "aliases": [], "description": "The ability to return to normal function after an unexpected adverse event.", "grounding_span": "resilience is the ability", "location": "MEASURE 2.7"},
    {"id": "c58", "name": "Adversarial examples", "aliases": [], "description": "A common security concern for AI systems involving crafted inputs.", "grounding_span": "Common security concerns relate to adversarial examples, data poisoning", "location": "MEASURE 2.7"},
    {"id": "c59", "name": "Data poisoning", "aliases": [], "description": "A security concern involving manipulation of training data.", "grounding_span": "adversarial examples, data poisoning", "location": "MEASURE 2.7"},
    {"id": "c60", "name": "Transparency", "aliases": [], "description": "Characteristic enabling meaningful visibility into AI pipelines; answers the question of 'what happened'.", "grounding_span": "Transparency can answer the question of \u201cwhat happened\u201d.", "location": "MEASURE 2.9"},
    {"id": "c61", "name": "Explainability", "aliases": [], "description": "Characteristic that answers the question of 'how' a decision was made in the system.", "grounding_span": "Explainability can", "location": "MEASURE 2.9"},
    {"id": "c62", "name": "Interpretability", "aliases": [], "description": "Characteristic that answers the question of 'why' a decision was made by the system and its meaning or context to the user.", "grounding_span": "Interpretability can", "location": "MEASURE 2.9"},
    {"id": "c63", "name": "Accountability", "aliases": [], "description": "Accountable leadership and transparent roles that foster and incentivize quality assurance and risk management.", "grounding_span": "Risks associated with transparency and accountability", "location": "MEASURE 2.8"},
    {"id": "c64", "name": "Privacy", "aliases": [], "description": "The norms and practices that help to safeguard human autonomy, identity, and dignity.", "grounding_span": "Privacy refers generally to the norms and practices that help to safeguard human autonomy,", "location": "MEASURE 2.10"},
    {"id": "c65", "name": "Privacy-enhancing technologies", "aliases": ["PETs"], "description": "Technologies and data-minimizing methods (e.g., de-identification, aggregation) that support privacy-enhanced AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Privacy-enhancing technologies (\u201cPETs\u201d) for AI", "location": "MEASURE 2.10"},
    {"id": "c66", "name": "Differential privacy", "aliases": [], "description": "A privacy-enhancing technique used when publicly sharing dataset information.", "grounding_span": "Use privacy-enhancing techniques such as differential privacy", "location": "MEASURE 2.10"},
    {"id": "c67", "name": "K-anonymity", "aliases": ["l-diversity", "t-closeness"], "description": "Metric quantifying privacy-level data aspects such as the ability to identify individuals or groups.", "grounding_span": "k-anonymity metrics, l-diversity, t-closeness", "location": "MEASURE 2.10"},
    {"id": "c68", "name": "Fairness", "aliases": [], "description": "Concern for equality and equity in AI, addressing harmful bias and discrimination.", "grounding_span": "Fairness in AI includes concerns for equality and equity", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"},
    {"id": "c69", "name": "Bias", "aliases": ["AI bias"], "description": "Broader than demographic balance; NIST identifies systemic, computational/statistical, and human-cognitive categories.", "grounding_span": "NIST has identified three major categories of AI bias", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"},
    {"id": "c70", "name": "Systemic bias", "aliases": [], "description": "Bias present in AI datasets, organizational norms/practices/processes, and the broader society that uses AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Systemic bias can be present in AI datasets", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"},
    {"id": "c71", "name": "Computational and statistical bias", "aliases": [], "description": "Bias present in datasets and algorithmic processes, often stemming from systematic errors due to non-representative samples.", "grounding_span": "Computational and statistical biases can be present in AI datasets and algorithmic", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"},
    {"id": "c72", "name": "Human-cognitive bias", "aliases": [], "description": "Bias relating to how an individual or group perceives AI system information to make a decision or fill in missing information.", "grounding_span": "Human-cognitive biases relate to how an individual or group perceives AI system", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"},
    {"id": "c73", "name": "Environmental impact", "aliases": ["sustainability"], "description": "Direct and indirect environmental impacts of AI model training and management activities.", "grounding_span": "Environmental impact and sustainability of AI model training", "location": "MEASURE 2.12"},
    {"id": "c74", "name": "Greenhouse gas emissions", "aliases": ["GHG emissions"], "description": "A type of direct negative environmental impact from AI computational resources.", "grounding_span": "greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions", "location": "MEASURE 2.12"},
    {"id": "c75", "name": "Rebound effects", "aliases": [], "description": "Indirect environmental effect where efficiency gains are offset by accelerated resource consumption.", "grounding_span": "\u201crebound effects\u201d, where efficiency gains are offset by accelerated", "location": "MEASURE 2.12"},
    {"id": "c76", "name": "Emergent risks", "aliases": [], "description": "Risks that may emerge over time or be difficult to measure, requiring systematic tracking approaches.", "grounding_span": "identify emergent risks", "location": "MEASURE 3.2"},
    {"id": "c77", "name": "Selective adherence", "aliases": [], "description": "A known human-AI configuration risk that system features and capabilities may activate.", "grounding_span": "such as selective adherence.", "location": "MAP 3.4"},
    {"id": "c78", "name": "Underspecification", "aliases": [], "description": "A failure mode difficult to identify using current standard AI testing processes.", "grounding_span": "shortcut learning or", "location": "GOVERN 4.3"},
    {"id": "c79", "name": "Value sensitive design", "aliases": ["VSD"], "description": "Method to identify misalignments between organizational and societal values and system implementation and impact.", "grounding_span": "Employ methods such as value sensitive design (VSD)", "location": "MAP 5.2"},
    {"id": "c80", "name": "Human-centered design", "aliases": ["HCD"], "description": "Approach helping AI teams engage broadly with individuals and communities to learn about positive or negative impacts.", "grounding_span": "human-centered design (HCD) and value-sensitive design (VSD)", "location": "MAP 3.1"},
    {"id": "c81", "name": "AI lifecycle", "aliases": [], "description": "The set of interdependent activities involving diverse actors spanning design through decommissioning of AI systems.", "grounding_span": "throughout the AI lifecycle", "location": "GOVERN 1.2"},
    {"id": "c82", "name": "AI actors", "aliases": [], "description": "Those who operate, use, interact with, oversee, or monitor AI systems across the lifecycle.", "grounding_span": "empowered AI actors", "location": "GOVERN 2.1"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d1", "term": "Disparate impact", "verbatim_text": "Disparate impact is broadly defined as a facially neutral policy or practice that disproportionately harms a group based on a protected trait.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "Disparate impact is broadly defined as a", "location": "GOVERN 1.1"},
    {"id": "d2", "term": "Negative residual risks", "verbatim_text": "Negative residual risks (defined as the sum of all unmitigated risks)", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "Negative residual risks (defined as the sum of all unmitigated risks)", "location": "MANAGE 1.4"},
    {"id": "d3", "term": "Risk", "verbatim_text": "Risk refers to the composite measure of an event\u2019s probability of occurring and the magnitude (or degree) of the consequences of the corresponding events.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "Risk refers to the composite measure of an event\u2019s probability of occurring and the", "location": "MANAGE 1.2"},
    {"id": "d4", "term": "Risk tolerance", "verbatim_text": "Risk tolerance reflects the level and type of risk the organization is willing to accept while conducting its mission and carrying out its strategy.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "Risk tolerance reflects the level and type of risk the organization is willing to accept while", "location": "MAP 1.5"},
    {"id": "d5", "term": "AI system inventory", "verbatim_text": "An AI system inventory is an organized database of artifacts relating to an AI system or model.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "An AI system inventory is an organized database of artifacts relating to an AI system or", "location": "GOVERN 1.6"},
    {"id": "d6", "term": "human-AI configurations", "verbatim_text": "human-AI configurations (configurations where AI systems are explicitly designated and treated as team members in primarily human teams)", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "human-AI configurations (configurations", "location": "GOVERN 3.2"},
    {"id": "d7", "term": "construct validity", "verbatim_text": "construct validity (the test  is measuring the concept it claims to measure)", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "construct validity (the test", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "d8", "term": "internal validity", "verbatim_text": "internal validity (relationship being tested is not influenced by other factors or variables)", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "internal validity (relationship being tested is not influenced by other factors or", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "d9", "term": "external validity", "verbatim_text": "external validity (results are generalizable beyond the training condition)", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "external validity (results are generalizable beyond the training condition)", "location": "MEASURE 2.5"},
    {"id": "d10", "term": "resilience", "verbatim_text": "resilience is the ability to return to normal function after an unexpected adverse event", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "resilience is the ability to return to normal function after an unexpected adverse event", "location": "MEASURE 2.7"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "Highly accurate and optimized systems can cause harm.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Highly accurate and optimized systems can cause harm.", "location": "MAP 1.1"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "Without such policies, risk management can be subjective across the organization, and exacerbate rather than minimize risks over time.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "risk management can be subjective across the organization, and exacerbate rather than minimize risks over time.", "location": "GOVERN 1.2"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "AI systems are dynamic and may perform in unexpected ways once deployed or after deployment.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "AI systems are dynamic and may perform in unexpected ways once deployed or after", "location": "GOVERN 1.5"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "The clearer and narrower the task definition, the easier it is to map its benefits and risks, leading to more fulsome risk management.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The clearer and narrower the", "location": "MAP 2.1"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "Systems in which harmful biases are mitigated are not necessarily fair.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Systems in which harmful biases are mitigated are not necessarily fair.", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "Systems that function in a narrow scope tend to enable better mapping, measurement, and management of risks.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Systems that function in a narrow scope tend to enable better mapping, measurement, and", "location": "MAP 3.3"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "The use of pre-trained models can make it difficult to anticipate negative system outcomes or impacts.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The use of pre-trained", "location": "MANAGE 3.2"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "AI systems can potentially increase the speed and scale of biases and perpetuate and amplify harms to individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "AI systems can potentially increase the speed and scale of biases and perpetuate and amplify harms to", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"},
    {"id": "cl9", "claim_text": "The current AI systems are brittle, the failure modes are not well described, and the systems are dependent on the context in which they were developed and do not transfer well outside of the training environment.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The current AI systems are brittle, the failure modes are not well described", "location": "MEASURE 1.3"},
    {"id": "cl10", "claim_text": "Transparency, explainability, and interpretability are distinct characteristics that support each other.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Transparency, explainability, and interpretability are distinct characteristics that support each other.", "location": "MEASURE 2.9"},
    {"id": "cl11", "claim_text": "Security and resilience are related but distinct characteristics.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Security and resilience are related but distinct characteristics.", "location": "MEASURE 2.7"},
    {"id": "cl12", "claim_text": "AI systems may not necessarily be the right solution for a given business task or problem.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "AI systems may not necessarily be the right solution for a given business task or problem.", "location": "MANAGE 1.1"},
    {"id": "cl13", "claim_text": "Under certain conditions such as data sparsity, privacy enhancing techniques can result in a loss in accuracy, impacting decisions about fairness and other values in certain domains.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "privacy enhancing techniques can result in a loss in accuracy", "location": "MEASURE 2.10"},
    {"id": "cl14", "claim_text": "Large-scale, high-performance computational resources used by AI systems for training and operation can contribute to environmental impacts.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Large-scale, high-performance computational resources used by AI systems for training and", "location": "MEASURE 2.12"}
  ],

  "measures": [
    {"id": "m1", "text": "Has the system been reviewed for its compliance to applicable laws, regulations, standards, and guidance?", "response_type": "yes/no", "grounding_span": "Has the system been reviewed for its compliance to applicable laws, regulations,", "location": "GOVERN 1.1"},
    {"id": "m2", "text": "To what extent has the entity clarified the roles, responsibilities, and delegated authorities to relevant stakeholders?", "response_type": "open-ended/degree", "grounding_span": "To what extent has the entity clarified the roles, responsibilities, and delegated", "location": "GOVERN 1.4"},
    {"id": "m3", "text": "Which population(s) does the AI system impact?", "response_type": "open-ended", "grounding_span": "Which population(s) does the AI system impact?", "location": "MAP 5.1"},
    {"id": "m4", "text": "Are greenhouse gas emissions, and energy and water consumption and efficiency tracked within the organization?", "response_type": "yes/no", "grounding_span": "Are greenhouse gas emissions, and energy and water consumption and efficiency", "location": "MEASURE 2.12"},
    {"id": "m5", "text": "Given the purpose of this AI, what is an appropriate interval for checking whether it is still accurate, unbiased, explainable, etc.?", "response_type": "open-ended", "grounding_span": "what is an appropriate interval for checking whether it is", "location": "MEASURE 2.1"},
    {"id": "m6", "text": "If human subjects were used in the development or testing of the AI system, what protections were put in place to promote their safety and wellbeing?", "response_type": "open-ended", "grounding_span": "If human subjects were used in the development or testing of the AI system, what", "location": "MEASURE 2.2"},
    {"id": "m7", "text": "Given the purpose of the AI, what level of explainability or interpretability is required for how the AI made its determination?", "response_type": "open-ended", "grounding_span": "Given the purpose of the AI, what level of explainability or interpretability is required", "location": "MEASURE 2.9"},
    {"id": "m8", "text": "To what extent has the entity identified and mitigated potential bias\u2014statistical, contextual, and historical\u2014in the data?", "response_type": "open-ended/degree", "grounding_span": "To what extent has the entity identified and mitigated potential bias\u2014statistical,", "location": "MEASURE 2.11"}
  ],

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "Suggestions are aligned to each sub-category within the four AI RMF", "suggested_edge": "aligned_to", "location": "About the Playbook", "note": "The Playbook's suggested actions are aligned to each AI RMF Core sub-category; no edge type expresses document-to-framework-subcategory alignment."},
    {"grounding_span": "security includes resilience", "suggested_edge": "encompasses", "location": "MEASURE 2.7", "note": "Security encompasses resilience; no part-whole edge fits a concept-encompasses-concept superset relationship distinct from has_component."},
    {"grounding_span": "risk \u2248 impact x likelihood", "suggested_edge": "computed_from", "location": "GOVERN 1.3", "note": "Risk is computed as a function of impact and likelihood; no edge type expresses a formula/derivation relationship between concepts."},
    {"grounding_span": "distinct characteristics that support each other", "suggested_edge": "supports", "location": "MEASURE 2.9", "note": "Transparency, explainability, and interpretability mutually support each other; no non-hierarchical 'supports' edge exists."},
    {"grounding_span": "output from the Map and Measure functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.", "suggested_edge": "consumes_output_of", "location": "MANAGE", "note": "Manage consumes analytical output of Map and Measure; precedes only expresses temporal ordering, not input/output dependency."}
  ]
